reasoning: The BAMLH0A0HYM2 OAS recently spiked from 2.64 to 2.88 towards the end of January, rising 24 bps in just over a week. Short-term rapid widenings without a severe macroeconomic catalyst often partially mean-revert as market conditions stabilize, implying a higher probability that the spread will decrease or stay relatively flat over the near term compared to 2.88. The FRED statistical tool generated a 0.29 probability of the spread increasing beyond 2.88 by next week, aligning with this mean-reversion expectation. Therefore, I estimate a 38% probability of an increase by February 8. Over the longer 1-month horizon to March 3, uncertainty increases. Furthermore, because spreads are still at historically very tight levels (2.88% compared to a historical median around 4.7%), there is a moderately asymmetric fundamental risk of spreads drifting wider over time. This pulls the March 3 probability closer to the base rate of 50%, resulting in an estimate of 47%.
